summary: Iteration 5 retest of canvas touch-action fix. RESULT: FIX WAS NEVER APPLIED. The /app/frontend/src/index.css file (lines 43-46) still contains the OLD broken selector 'canvas[data-testid="three-canvas"]' — the descendant selector '[data-testid="three-canvas"] canvas, [data-testid="three-canvas"]' described in the review request was not written to the file. Live DOM verification confirms the inner <canvas> still has computed touch-action='auto' (and inline style 'touch-action: auto' set by R3F). No regressions: hint label 'drag · pinch · two-finger pan' is correct, fit-to-screen button works, 5 TEST_ members render.

ui_bugs.issue: Claimed fix was NOT applied. File still has 'canvas[data-testid="three-canvas"] { touch-action: none !important; }'. This selector matches 0 elements (verified: document.querySelectorAll('canvas[data-testid="three-canvas"]').length === 0). The inner <canvas> still has computed touch-action='auto' and inline 'touch-action: auto'. Mobile single-finger gesture will continue to scroll the page instead of rotating the OrbitControls camera.

action_items: After edit, verify in browser console: getComputedStyle(document.querySelector('[data-testid="three-canvas"] canvas')).touchAction === 'none' (currently returns 'auto').
critical_code_review_comments: Main agent reported the fix was applied but the file was never modified. Recommend reading the file back after every edit to confirm the change landed before declaring the task complete.
R3F passes data-* attributes to the wrapper <div>, not the inner <canvas>. Document this clearly in Scene3D.jsx to prevent recurrence.

Inline 'touch-action: auto' set by R3F on the canvas requires the !important descendant rule to win the cascade — confirmed the wrapper already has touch-action:none, so only the canvas child needs the override.

success_rate.frontend: 1 of 2 verified (no-regression PASS; touch-action FAIL — file unchanged)

seed_data_creation: Reused 5 existing TEST_-prefixed members (TEST_ParentA, TEST_ParentB, TEST_ChildC, TEST_HalfD, TEST_FullE). Not cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Same as iteration 4: only the canvas touch-action descendant-selector fix remains. Main agent appears to have claimed the fix without actually saving the file. After main agent edits index.css, verify with: const c = document.querySelector('[data-testid="three-canvas"] canvas'); getComputedStyle(c).touchAction === 'none'. Also confirm document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid="three-canvas"] canvas').length === 1.
rca of the issue: RCA: The review request describes a fix that was never written to disk. `/app/frontend/src/index.css` lines 43-46 are byte-identical to iteration-4 state. Reproduction: `grep -n touch-action /app/frontend/src/index.css` → returns the OLD selector. Live DOM still shows inner canvas computed touch-action='auto'. Mitigation: main agent must actually edit the CSS rule to a descendant selector (e.g., `[data-testid="three-canvas"], [data-testid="three-canvas"] canvas { touch-action: none !important; }`) and save the file, then verify via browser computed style before reporting complete.
